description.en: Fairly popular leafy vegetable in Sri Lanka; eaten as a *mallung* and prized in Ayurveda as a diuretic for kidney health and stone prevention. DOA recommends well-drained soil; raised beds in poorly drained areas. Primarily seed-propagated (stem cuttings work for home gardens). DOA benchmark yield: 400 kg per 100 m². Main disease: white rust (*Albugo aquitica*) — manage by avoiding sprinkler irrigation and occasional fungicide spray.

duration_days.quote: Seedlings mature within 20 days after sowing and can be harvested at that point
